results.change_condition: for this sequence number encryption procedure
results.new_value: length must be at least 16 bytes
results.extracted_evidence: This procedure requires the ciphertext length to be at least 16 bytes.
results.standard_text: This procedure requires the ciphertext length to be at least 16 bytes. Receivers MUST reject shorter records as if they had failed deprotection. Senders MUST pad short plaintexts out (using the conventional record padding mechanism) in order to make a suitable-length ciphertext.

results.change_condition: A server believes it has an existing association on a given host/port quartet and it receives a new handshake appearing as a ClientHello with epoch=0
results.change_action: must equal 0 for the described new handshake case
results.related_state_or_step: establishing new associations with existing parameters
results.extracted_evidence: This will appear to the server as a new handshake with epoch=0.  In cases where a server believes it has an existing association on a given host/port quartet and it receives an epoch=0 ClientHello, it SHOULD proceed with a new handshake
results.standard_text: The epoch number is initially zero; the DTLSPlaintext epoch is set as the least significant 2 bytes of the connection epoch; the unified header E bits carry the low-order two bits of the epoch; epoch values are assigned to unencrypted, early data, handshake, and application traffic secrets.

results.change_condition: After successful ACK processing of a KeyUpdate
results.extracted_evidence: Figure 14 shows an example exchange illustrating that successful ACK processing updates the keys of the KeyUpdate message sender, which is reflected in the change of epoch values.
results.standard_section: RFC 9147 Sections 5.8 and 7.1, KeyUpdate and ACK
results.standard_text: As with other handshake messages with no built-in response, KeyUpdates MUST be acknowledged. Implementations MUST NOT send records with the new keys or send a new KeyUpdate until the previous KeyUpdate has been acknowledged.

results.change_action: validated range check; application data records for epoch 3 and above MUST either be discarded or buffered

results.extracted_evidence: Implementations MUST either discard or buffer all application data records for epoch 3 and above until they have received the Finished message from the peer.

results.change_condition: Due to loss and/or reordering, when receiving a record
results.change_action: must be validated as not newer than the current one; older value may be processed or discarded
results.extracted_evidence: DTLS 1.3 implementations may receive a record with an older epoch than the current one (the requirements above preclude receiving a newer record). They SHOULD attempt to process those records with that epoch ... but MAY opt to discard such out-of-epoch records.

results.change_condition: after the handshake
results.change_action: must use the highest available sending epoch
results.extracted_evidence: After the handshake, implementations MUST use the highest available sending epoch.
results.standard_text: During the handshake, ACK records MUST be sent with an epoch which is equal to or higher than the record which is being acknowledged. After the handshake, implementations MUST use the highest available sending epoch. Implementations SHOULD simply use the highest current sending epoch.

results.change_condition: client receives only the ServerHello and Certificate and wishes to ACK them in a single record
results.change_action: set to epoch 2 or greater; cannot use any greater epoch not yet available

results.change_condition: until the previous KeyUpdate has been acknowledged
results.change_action: must not send records with new value
results.extracted_evidence: implementations MUST NOT send records with the new keys or send a new KeyUpdate until the previous KeyUpdate has been acknowledged
results.standard_section: RFC 9147 Sections 5.8 and 7.1, KeyUpdate and ACK
results.standard_text: As with other handshake messages with no built-in response, KeyUpdates MUST be acknowledged. Implementations MUST NOT send records with the new keys or send a new KeyUpdate until the previous KeyUpdate has been acknowledged.

results.change_condition: when a record is received with an epoch value for which no corresponding cipher state can be determined
results.extracted_evidence: Implementations that receive a record with an epoch value for which no corresponding cipher state can be determined SHOULD handle it as a record which fails deprotection.
results.standard_section: RFC 9147 Section 4.2.2, Reconstructing the Sequence Number and Epoch
results.standard_text: After the handshake is complete, if the epoch bits do not match those from the current epoch, implementations SHOULD use the most recent past epoch which has matching bits.

results.change_condition: when the client receives HelloRetryRequest containing a cookie
results.change_action: must be present / cookie added as an extension
results.extracted_evidence: The client MUST send a new ClientHello with the cookie added as an extension.
results.standard_text: The client MUST send a new ClientHello with the cookie added as an extension. The server then verifies the cookie and proceeds with the handshake only if it is valid. A DTLS 1.3-only client MUST set the legacy_cookie field to zero length.

results.change_condition: for the unified header
results.extracted_evidence: Fixed Bits:  The three high bits of the first byte of the unified header are set to 001.
results.standard_text: Fixed Bits: The three high bits of the first byte of the unified header are set to 001. If the first byte is any other value, then receivers MUST check to see if the leading bits of the first byte are 001. Otherwise, the record MUST be rejected as if it had failed deprotection.
